feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: unable get email to set up password after i subscribed cloud identity services on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am using an SAP BTP Trial account and have successfully subscribed to Cloud Identity Services. But I am not getting email to set up my password.

I can log in to the SAP BTP Trial Cockpit using my registered email address without any issues. However, when I click "Go to Application" for Cloud Identity Services, I am redirected to the sign-in page and receive the error:

"Sorry, we couldn't authenticate you."

I have already tried the following:

  • Resetting my password in the Identity Authentication tenant

  • Logging out and logging back in

  • Using the same email address that works for the BTP Trial Cockpit

Thanks,

Gayathri.

entry.title: I never received onboarding email Activate Your Account for Identity Authentication Service for CIS
entry.content:
I am using a BTP trail account  and i " I never received the "Activate Your Account for Identity Authentication Service" onboarding email for the first administrator.  "  for the Cloud Identity Services.

I also tried to reset the Password , but never received an email to reset.

Can you please help to resolve this issue?
 
Appreciate your help

entry.title: User Profile Not Found - Story Report
entry.content: Your Profile is not correctly configured for story reports. This can be a user sync issue.

entry.title: BTP Trial Registration - Phone Verification Blocked
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

I am trying to register for an SAP BTP Trial account so I can begin my hands-on learning, but I am completely stuck at the telephone number verification step. Every time I input my information and request the verification code, I receive this exact error message:

"We did not send you a code because you are not allowed to request phone calls. Please contact your system administrator."

Because this is a personal trial account for learning purposes, I do not have a system administrator to contact.

My Location Details:

  • Country: Philippines

  • Country Code: +63

(Note: The email address associated with my stuck registration is the same one I am using to post this community question. I can provide my full phone number via Direct Message to an SAP Moderator if needed.)

Troubleshooting Already Attempted:

  • Switched to a mobile hotspot network to change my IP/routing.

  • Cleared browser cache, cookies, and tried Incognito/Private browsing.

  • Verified that my phone number formatting is correct and completed the CAPTCHA successfully.

Could an SAP moderator please assist me with overriding or whitelisting this registration from the backend so I can access the BTP Cockpit?

Thank you!

entry.title: How to Automatically Assign Default Business Roles to New Users in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and manage users through the Maintain Business Users application.

We have a requirement to automatically assign a set of default business roles whenever a new business user is created. Currently, the role assignment is performed manually, which becomes time-consuming and prone to errors when onboarding a large number of users.

We would like to understand:

  • Is there any standard API available to assign business roles to users in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud?
  • Is there a way to automate business role assignment during or after user creation?
  • Are there any recommended approaches using SAP Cloud Identity Services, SAP Integration Suite, SAP BTP, or other SAP-supported integration options?
  • Has anyone implemented a similar solution in a Public Cloud environment?

Our objective is to ensure that newly created users automatically receive a predefined set of business roles without requiring manual intervention.

Any guidance, best practices, or references to relevant SAP documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Siva Nagi Reddy

entry.title: MyAccess supports SCIM APIs
entry.content: We want to setup a Database (MyAccess) as the source system for IAS user store using IPS. Client wants to use an API to do send the data from MyAccess to CIS user store. Is this feasible and if yes, could you please guide us with the process to do this.

entry.title: Basic Authentication no longer work after November 2026
entry.content:
As we recently got the message in the system.

"You use Basic Authentication method to connect to Employee Central. This method will no longer work after November 2026. Please check KBA 3705459"

Would it be possible to get more information about this for our internal preparation.

entry.title: IAS Configuration related queries
entry.content:
Hi team,

Hope you are doing well!

We need your help to understand the below points regarding IAS/IPS configurations

1) We need to configure email templates for onboarding in 7+ languages. We understand that the templates must be created using both plain text and HTML formats. Do we need to manually create these templates in both formats for all languages, or is there any alternative approach available?

2) We understand that Locale and Country data can be synced from SuccessFactors to IAS. However, for email template creation, we need to trigger emails based on country-specific conditions. For example, if the country is the Netherlands, the email language should be Dutch, and if the country is Malaysia, the email language should be Malay. Is this achievable by configuring multiple languages within the same template set? Currently, in onboarding, the locale for all users is defaulted to en_US. We need to assign the locale within the IAS system based on the country. Could you please assist with this?

3) We would like to sync a custom HRIS field (Employee Group) from Job Information (Malaysia) in SuccessFactors to the IAS system to filter email templates. Could you please confirm whether it is possible to sync a custom field to IAS? If yes, kindly share any relevant KBA or documentation on how to achieve this.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: HTTP Status 404 – Access to this SAP Cloud Platform Identity Authentication tenant is blocked.
entry.content:
Hi, after i subscribed cloud identity services on BTP, i got email with activation link to reset password. but when i am trying to access, i am getting below error message.

"HTTP Status 404 – Access to this SAP Cloud Platform Identity Authentication tenant is blocked, please contact your administrator."

Can anyone help here.

Thanks,

Gayathri.

entry.title: JOULE ARIBA - SSO
entry.content:
Hi SAP

Here's a summary of what we're doing:

Steps 1 and 3 of the following SAP Community blog post, regarding the integration of Joule with Ariba Sourcing, will be followed:Joule Integration with SAP Ariba - Setup Guide - SAP Community

(configuring Ariba SSO with Cloud Identity Services and configuring the Identity Provisioning Service between Ariba and IAS, synchronizing users between both applications). Step 2 of the blog post (obtaining OAuth credentials for the CDM API) has already been completed.

For step 1 mentioned above, the instructions in this other SAP Community blog post will be followed: Configure Single-Sign-On for SAP Ariba with SAP Cl... - SAP Community

 

Best Regards.

Hernan

entry.title: Cloud Identity Services Trial Admin Console Returns forbidden.html
entry.content:
I created a new SAP BTP Trial account and subscribed to Cloud Identity Services (default plan).

The tenant was created successfully and I can log in. Profile Management works correctly.

However, when I open the Administration Console (either from BTP Cockpit → Go to Application or directly via https://.trial-accounts.ondemand.com/admin), I am redirected to:

/admin/forbidden.html

Additional information:

  • User has Subaccount Administrator role.

  • Trust Configuration is healthy.

  • Administration Console application is shown as ACTIVE for the user.

  • Profile Management works.

  • Trial account was created on June 10, 2026.

Expected result:
Administration Console opens successfully.

Actual result:
forbidden.html is displayed.

Has anyone seen this with a newly created trial tenant, and is there a way to repair the administrator assignment without recreating the tenant?

entry.title: Cloud Identity Services — Admin Console always redirects to forbidden.html
entry.content:
[BTP Trial] Cloud Identity Services — Admin Console always redirects to forbidden.html after activation

Environment
- SAP BTP Trial Account
- Service: Cloud Identity Services (CIS)
- Region: cf-eu10

Problem Description

After subscribing to Cloud Identity Services in my BTP trial subaccount, I receive the activation email ("Activate Your Account for Identity Authentication Service"), click the link, and successfully set a password. The account activation completes without errors.

However, when I try to access the IAS Admin Console at:
I am always redirected to:

https://<tenant-id>.trial-accounts.ondemand.com/admin/forbidden.html
With the message: *"You entered a valid URL, but you are not authorized to view the content; contact your system administrator"*.

**What I have already tried**

1. Checked BTP Cockpit → Subaccount → Security → Roles: only Connectivity roles appear, no Cloud Identity Services roles — confirming the CIS roles were not provisioned.
2. Tried resetting password via "Forgot Password" — blocked by 24h cooldown after a previous attempt.
3. Tried accessing `https://iamtenants.accounts.cloud.sap` — returns HTTP 500 (expected, as this requires an S-User which is not available on trial).
4. **Deleted the BTP trial account entirely and created a brand new one  exact same issue reproduced from scratch on the new tenant

entry.title: showing access denied issue while trying to alunch SAP Cloud Identity Service
entry.content: After i setup my password, i am trying to launch Sap Cloud identity Service getting Access Denied error

entry.title: Feasibility & Architecture: Centralized Governance for Hybrid .NET (Azure) with IAG, SCIM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am designing a centralized Identity and Access Management (IAM) and governance strategy for a hybrid multi-cloud landscape, and I want to validate the absolute technical feasibility and best practices for this setup.

Our Target Requirement

We aim to establish a centralized administration system to manage, provision, and audit user identities, roles, and access across both our core SAP landscape (S/4HANA Cloud, SAP BTP) and non-SAP custom applications ( .NET apps and Java apps).

The primary business and compliance goals are:

  1. Single Administration Platform: All user lifecycle adjustments (onboarding, offboarding, modifications) must be handled from a single platform.

  2. Unified Access Governance: Centralized execution of workflows, risk analytics/Segregation of Duties (SoD) checks, and compliant access provisioning across SAP and non-SAP boundaries.

  3. Dynamic API Authorization: For our custom .NET app on Azure, authentication will run via Azure AD (Microsoft Entra ID), but granular authorization (CRUD validations) must map dynamically to assigned enterprise role metrics.

Proposed High-Level Architecture Component Layout

  • Access Governance: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (IAG).

  • Identity Lifecycle & Provisioning Engine: SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Provisioning (IPS).

  • Identity Federation / Token Proxy: SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication (IAS) mapped to SAP BTP XSUAA.

  • Non-SAP Endpoint target: Custom .NET apps, Java apps.

 

Our Specific Queries for SAP Experts

1. Architectural Feasibility & Non-SAP App Connectors

  • Is it fully feasible to utilize SAP IAG as the master governance authority to provision and control user entitlements for a standalone custom web API deployed outside the SAP perimeter (Azure)?

  • Given that IAG natively supports standard SAP Cloud applications out-of-the-box (e.g., S/4HANA Cloud, Ariba, SuccessFactors), what is the recommended design pattern to register an external custom .NET endpoint as a recognized Target System within the governance framework?

2. Where Exactly Does SCIM 2.0 Apply?

  • We intend to use the System for Cross-Domain Identity Management (SCIM 2.0) protocol standard. Does SAP IAG communicate identity schemas directly via SCIM, or does it orchestrate the target provisioning lifecycle implicitly by delegating to SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) as its execution engine?

  • To map custom security attributes to our .NET app (e.g., distinguishing between a view-only Viewer and a read-write Admin), should the SCIM 2.0 target schema extension configuration reside inside SAP IPS transformations or directly in the IAS identity directory configuration?

3. Necessary Customization within SAP IAG

  • What specific configurations or customizations are required within SAP IAG (such as the Access Request or Role Design modules) to ingest custom repository boundaries, define non-SAP technical permission roles, and run Segregation of Duties (SoD) risk analysis over custom application actions?

  • How do we construct custom repository structures within IAG when the target system does not offer a native metadata connector endpointt

I would appreciate any insights, architectural blueprints, or implementation guidance from anyone who has deployed a similar hybrid infrastructure using SAP IAG/IPS for third-party endpoints.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: My Profile button in Success Factors
entry.content:
Good morning,

We kindly request your assistance in clarifying why there are different names on the profile button for the employee themselves, depending on whether or not they are a manager. If the user is a manager, the button shows “Employee File”; if not, it shows “My Profile.” How can we change this?

Thank you.

entry.title: SID Deactivate in Public Cloud/IAS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with IAS/CBC and S4HC. For employee offboarding, we are considering the following process to deactive SID instead of delete SID, for we are afraid if we delete, it will influence IAS/S4 system inconsistent.

Process as following:

1. Set user status to Inactive in IAS

Status: Inactive
Valid To: End of Valid Date
(eg 2026/06/16)

2. Remove user groups in IAS

All unassign(clear all group)

3. Synchronize changes to CBC in IAS(IPS)

4. Maintain Worker work type valid date in APP "Manage Workforce" in S/4HANA Cloud
Worker Types End date:
End of Valid Date
(eg. 2026/06/16)

5. Lock the business user in APP "Maintain Business User" in S/4HANA Cloud

Click "Lock" indicator

6. Remove all assigned business roles in APP "Maintain Business User" in S/4HANA Cloud

Remove all Business Role

Q1: For sure following step is correct or SAP recommended approach is delete SID? 

Q2: If you complete the steps above, the SID account will no longer occupy FUE?

Thanks.

Tina

entry.title: Subject: Unable to Access SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) Administration Console – /admin/forbidde
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am facing an issue while accessing the SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) Administration Console in an SAP BTP Trial account.

Steps Performed:

  1. Created a new SAP BTP Trial account.

  2. Created a new subaccount.

  3. Assigned administrator role collections to my user, including:

    • Subaccount Administrator

    • Subaccount Service Administrator

  4. Added the Cloud Identity Services entitlement.

  5. Subscribed to Cloud Identity Services using the default plan.

  6. Received the "Welcome to SAP Cloud Identity Services" activation email.

  7. Clicked the activation link and successfully activated the account.

  8. Set the password successfully.

  9. Configured Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) successfully.

  10. Opened Cloud Identity Services using "Go To Application".

Trust Configuration Tests:

  • Tested with the Default Identity Provider (Active).

  • Also configured and tested with a Custom Identity Provider.

  • The same issue occurs in both scenarios.

Expected Result:

I should be able to access the IAS Administration Console.

Actual Result:

After successful authentication and MFA configuration, I am redirected to:

/admin/forbidden.html

The page displays the following message:

"Forbidden"

"Sorry, but you are not authorized to access the requested content."

"You entered a valid URL, but you are not authorized to view the content. Please contact your administrator for more information."

The URL is successfully reached, but access to the IAS Administration Console is denied.

Additional Information:

  • Authentication is successful.

  • MFA setup is successful.

  • Cloud Identity Services subscription status is "Subscribed".

  • BTP administrator role collections are assigned to my user.

  • The issue occurs after successful account activation, password setup, MFA configuration, and login.

  • The same issue occurs in two different SAP BTP Trial accounts created with two different email addresses.

  • The issue persists regardless of whether the Default Identity Provider or a Custom Identity Provider is configured.

Additional Observation:

Since the activation email is received, the account is activated successfully, MFA is configured successfully, and login succeeds, authentication appears to be working correctly. However, access to the IAS Administration Console is denied with /admin/forbidden.html.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Display access to Provisioning logs in Cloud Identity Services
entry.content: I am exploring the feasibility of providing display (read-only) access to provisioning logs in SAP Cloud Identity Services. Currently, SAP does not offer a dedicated read-only sub-role specifically for log access; however, is it possible to design a custom role to meet this requirement?Please advise

entry.title: Unable to Access Cloud Identity Services Admin Console – "You are not authorized to view the content
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am using an SAP BTP Trial account and have recently set up Cloud Identity Services.

The steps I completed are:

Created an SAP BTP Trial account and subaccount.
Enabled Cloud Identity Services from the BTP cockpit.
Established trust between the BTP subaccount and Cloud Identity Services.
Received the activation email and successfully activated the Cloud Identity Services tenant using the activation link.

Issue: After activation, when I am redirected to the Cloud Identity Services administration portal, I receive the following error message:

"You are not authorized to view the content. Please contact your administrator."

In some cases, the page also displays a "Forbidden" error.

The authentication process itself appears to complete successfully, and I can log in with my credentials. However, after authentication, I am unable to access the administration console.

What is confusing is that I am the administrator who activated the tenant from the activation email, so I expected to have administrative access to the Cloud Identity Services tenant.

Additional Information:

Environment: SAP BTP Trial
Cloud Identity Services tenant activated successfully
Trust configuration completed in BTP
Issue occurs immediately after authentication and redirection to the admin console
Has anyone experienced a similar issue with a trial account? Is there an additional role assignment or configuration step required after tenant activation?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you

entry.title: Trial Cloud Identity Service instance: access denied
entry.content:
I'm getting access denied error when I try to open the CIS tenant launched in the BTP trial account. It's been failing for more than a week. I've already tried to recreate the instance, but it doesn't work.

Can anyone help fix this issue or at least point me to the right channel to report BTP trial account issues?

 

 

Thanks.
